dashboard.label: T1568.002 — DGA C2
dashboard.description: Detects DGA-driven C2 (Conficker, Necurs, Murofet) by flagging hosts that fire many high-entropy base-domain queries with majority NXDOMAIN.

search.query: `dga_c2_lookup` | where like(src, "$src_filter$") OR "$src_filter$"="*" | stats avg(nxdomain_fraction) AS frac | eval frac=round(frac, 2) | table frac

`dga_c2_lookup` | where like(src, "$src_filter$") OR "$src_filter$"="*" | eval window=strftime(_time, "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M") | sort -distinct_domains | table window, src, distinct_domains, total_queries, nxdomain_fraction, avg_uniq_chars, sample_domains

index=zeek sourcetype=zeek:dns | eval is_nx=if(rcode_name="NXDOMAIN", 1, 0) | bin _time span=5m | stats count AS total, sum(is_nx) AS nx BY _time, src | eval frac=case(total>0, nx/total, true(), 0) | where total >= 5 | timechart span=5m avg(frac) BY src limit=8

html.p: Conficker, Necurs, Murofet, and many banking trojans use a deterministic seed (often the date) to compute a daily list of domain candidates. The implant tries each one until something resolves; the operator only needs to pre-register one. The defender sees a single host firing tens-to-hundreds of unique base-domain queries with most resolving NXDOMAIN.
The detection requires all of: ≥ 30 distinct base domains in a 5-minute window, average second-level-label unique-char count ≥ 8 (entropy proxy), and NXDOMAIN fraction ≥ 0.5.
